pattern: Two numbers multiply to a perfect square exactly when their squarefree parts match, so reducing each value to its squarefree core turns the ancestor test into counting equal cores along the root path during one DFS.
hints: A product is a perfect square iff every prime appears an even number of times; strip each value down to the product of primes that appear an odd number of times.
Once each node carries that squarefree signature, node i and an ancestor form a square product iff their signatures are identical.

DFS from the root keeping a frequency map of the signatures on the current root-to-node path; at each node add the count of its own signature already on the path, then push it and recurse, popping on the way back up.

edge_inputs.description: chain where each pair multiplies to a square
all ones make every product a perfect square
perfect squares all share signature one

stress.description: random tree with small values so squarefree collisions are frequent
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    edges = [[rng.randint(0, i - 1), i] for i in range(1, n)]
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [n, edges, nums]
stress.note: small value range keeps signatures colliding to stress the counting
